introSection: X Coaster is a Maurer AG built sit-down roller coaster located at Magic Springs in Hot Springs, Arkansas, USA. The ride was installed by Martin & Vleminckx.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The addition of X Coaster was announced on September 19, 2005.

When X Coaster opened on May 27, 2006, it became the park's 6th roller coaster and the first and only SkyLoop to be built in North America. X Coaster did not operate during the 2009 season, but was running again for 2010.

On October 5, 2012, Magic Springs and Crystal Falls announced the removal of the roller coaster at the end of the 2012 season to make way for expansion plans for the Crystal Falls water park for the 2013 season. However, the ride was not removed and continues to operate.

sections.title: Accidents
sections.content: On June 9, 2007, 12 riders were stranded upside down in the air for 30 minutes due to a power cut.

fullText:
X Coaster is a Maurer AG built sit-down roller coaster located at Magic Springs in Hot Springs, Arkansas, USA. The ride was installed by Martin & Vleminckx.

The addition of X Coaster was announced on September 19, 2005.

When X Coaster opened on May 27, 2006, it became the park's 6th roller coaster and the first and only SkyLoop to be built in North America. X Coaster did not operate during the 2009 season, but was running again for 2010.

On October 5, 2012, Magic Springs and Crystal Falls announced the removal of the roller coaster at the end of the 2012 season to make way for expansion plans for the Crystal Falls water park for the 2013 season. However, the ride was not removed and continues to operate.





On June 9, 2007, 12 riders were stranded upside down in the air for 30 minutes due to a power cut.
